sound_type: kill-dee alarm -- loud, insistent, repeated 'kill-DEE! kill-DEE!' that is both primary vocalization and source of common name; piercing, far-carrying; E=5
ecosystem: Open ground -- parking lots, gravel rooftops, agricultural fields, pastures, mudflats, gravel roads, lawns. Remarkably adaptable. Nests on open ground in human-modified and natural habitats.
season: Year-round resident in PNW. Nesting spring/summer. Kill-dee alarm given year-round. Double-brooding.

coordinates.description: Killdeer on open ground throughout the PNW -- parking lots, agricultural fields, gravel rooftops, mudflats, lawns, construction sites.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST CHARADRIIDAE entry. Plover family enters the engine. Most famous bird deception (broken-wing display). Species name vociferus means 'vociferous.' Common name is onomatopoeia. Linnaeus 1758.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Charadrius from Late Latin 'charadrius' (a yellowish bird). vociferus from Latin 'vox' (voice) + 'ferre' (to carry) -- vociferous, loud-voiced. Killdeer is onomatopoeia of the call.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Charadrius -- large genus of small plovers. ~30 species. Open-habitat specialists worldwide.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Charadriiformes. Third Charadriiformes entry in sequence (Haematopodidae 166, Charadriidae 167). Shorebird order expanding.
taxonomy_note.family_position: First Charadriidae. The vociferous plover. Broken-wing actor. E=5.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: Kill-dee alarm: 2500-4500 Hz. High-pitched, piercing, designed to carry across open ground.
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: 3000-4000 Hz. The stressed 'DEE' syllable carries the most energy.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual calls: 0.3-0.5 seconds. Sequences: 10-100+ repetitions. Minutes of continuous calling.
song_metrics.note_structure: Kill-DEE! Kill-DEE! -- two-syllable, emphasis on second syllable. Repeated rapidly. Also 'dee-dee-dee' variants. Display call more plaintive. Flight call rising.
song_metrics.note_types: Kill-dee alarm (primary -- alarm/territorial/general), display call (plaintive variant during broken-wing act), trill (courtship), flight call (rising dee-dee-dee).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Rapid repetition of kill-dee. Insistent. Can be continuous for minutes. Accelerates during alarm. Graduated intensity.
song_metrics.timbre: Shrill, piercing, clear. Designed for open-ground propagation. High-frequency cutting through ambient noise.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Loud for a shorebird. Carries well across parking lots, fields, mudflats. Insistent enough to be impossible to ignore.
song_metrics.tempo: Rapid. 2-4 kill-dee calls per second during alarm. Accelerates with increasing threat.
song_metrics.context: Predator alarm. Territorial defense. Broken-wing display accompaniment. Flight. General disturbance. Very easily triggered.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Kill-dee call consistent across range.
song_metrics.key_property: KILL-DEE ALARM -- loud, insistent, onomatopoeia. FIRST CHARADRIIDAE. Broken-wing display. Vociferus. Open ground nester. E=5. v1.49.307.

plumage.overall: Brown above, white below. Two black breast bands (diagnostic). White forehead and eyebrow. Orange-rufous rump visible in flight. Red eye-ring.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Two black breast bands on white chest. Red eye-ring. Orange rump in flight. Open-ground habitat.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. Two breast bands visible year-round.
plumage.flight_pattern: Strong, direct. Orange rump flash. Often calling in flight. Stiff wingbeats.

physical_description.overall: Length: 23-27 cm. Weight: 75-110g. Medium-sized plover. Long tail. Long legs for a plover.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Two black breast bands. Red eye-ring. On open ground.
physical_description.field_id: Brown-backed shorebird on open ground (parking lots, fields). Two black chest bands. Loud, insistent kill-DEE! kill-DEE! call. If it performs a broken-wing display, it's a Killdeer.

nodes.title: Kill-Dee Alarm -- The Vociferous Call
nodes.description: Loud, insistent, repeated kill-DEE! kill-DEE! 2500-4500 Hz. Piercing. Carries across open ground. Onomatopoeia = common name. Vociferus = species name. Both names reference the voice. E=5.

nodes.title: Broken-Wing Display -- Most Famous Bird Deception
nodes.description: Feigns injury (dragging wing) to lure predators from nest. Graduated response -- intensity matches threat. Effective against diverse predators. Fixed action pattern modified by context. Cultural icon of animal deception.

nodes.title: Open Ground Nesting
nodes.description: Scrape in gravel, no concealment. Parking lots, rooftops, construction sites. Cryptic eggs. Precocial chicks (running within hours). Defense entirely behavioral (broken-wing + alarm). Double-brooding.

nodes.title: First Charadriidae -- Plover Family Enters
nodes.description: Charadriidae enters at degree 167. 67 species worldwide. Plovers, lapwings, dotterels. Short stout bills. Run-stop-peck foraging. Open habitat specialists. Killdeer most adaptable.

nodes.title: Vociferus -- Named for the Voice
nodes.description: Both names reference the call. Killdeer = onomatopoeia. Charadrius vociferus = vociferous plover (loud-voiced). Linnaeus 1758. Accurate for 270 years. Few species so completely defined by their sound.

cross_references.relationship: CHARADRIIFORMES SEQUENCE! Oystercatcher (166) to plover (167). Rocky shore to open ground. Both loud. Same order, different habitats.
OPEN LANDSCAPE VOCALISTS! Crane: open field, bugle, E=6. Killdeer: open ground, kill-dee, E=5. Both loudly conspicuous in open spaces.
VISIBILITY CONTRAST! Mountain Quail: heard never seen (dense brush). Killdeer: heard AND seen (open ground). Opposite concealment.
PREDATOR DEFENSE! Spruce Grouse: freeze, camouflage (passive). Killdeer: perform, deceive (active). Opposite defense strategies.
